further_reading.title: OECD AI Principles Overview
further_reading.why_relevant: Provides a widely used high-level definition of an AI system and frames AI in terms of predictions, content, recommendations, and decisions.

further_reading.title: What is AI? Can you make a clear distinction between AI and non-AI systems?
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains the OECD AI-system definition in plainer language, including how machine learning differs from explicit hand-written rules.

further_reading.title: Does ChatGPT tell the truth?
further_reading.why_relevant: Gives a beginner-friendly explanation of hallucinations and why fluent model outputs still need verification.

notes: No inline citation seed entries were generated because this module is primarily pedagogical framing, analogies, and practice prompts, and it avoids the main citation-trigger classes called out by policy: named incidents, dated statistics, pricing, named standards or regulations, version-specific vendor claims, and security claims tied to real events.
